title: C Function Pointers
content:
### Function Pointers in C

A function pointer stores the address of a function, allowing functions to be passed as arguments or stored in arrays.

### Declaration

```c
return_type (*pointer_name)(parameter_types);
```

```c
int (*operation)(int, int);
```

### Assignment and Call

```c
int add(int a, int b) { return a + b; }
operation = &add;
int result = operation(10, 20);  // or (*operation)(10, 20)
```

### Use Cases

| Use Case | Example |
|----------|---------|
| Callbacks | Pass function to qsort() |
| Strategy pattern | Select algorithm at runtime |
| State machines | Function pointer tables |
| Event handlers | Register event callbacks |

### Array of Function Pointers

```c
int (*operations[])(int, int) = {add, subtract, multiply};
operations[choice](a, b);
```

### Typedef for Function Pointers

```c
typedef int (*MathOp)(int, int);
MathOp op = add;
```

---

> **Key Point:** Function pointers enable dynamic dispatch and callback mechanisms in C. They are essential for implementing event-driven and plugin architectures.
